name: Australia-Japan Cable
description: Cable connecting Australia to Japan via Guam

name: PIPE Pacific Cable-1
description: Cable connecting Australia to Guam and Hawaii

name: Tasman Global Access
description: Cable connecting Australia and New Zealand

name: SEA-ME-WE 3
description: Southeast Asia - Middle East - Western Europe 3, connecting Asia to Europe

name: SEA-ME-WE 4
description: Southeast Asia - Middle East - Western Europe 4, connecting Asia to Europe

name: Asia America Gateway
description: Cable connecting Southeast Asia to the United States via Guam and Hawaii

name: Indigo Central
description: Cable connecting Australia to Singapore via Indonesia

name: Indigo West
description: Cable connecting Australia to India via Singapore

name: Asia Pacific Gateway
description: Cable connecting Japan, Korea, Taiwan, Hong Kong, and Southeast Asia

name: East Asia Crossing
description: Cable connecting Japan, Korea, and Southeast Asia

name: Terrestrial
description: Terrestrial fibre segments

name: City to City
description: Pacnet ring cable system connecting major East Asian cities: Hong Kong, Manila, Taipei, Tokyo, Seoul with branch to Singapore

name: BiFrost
description: Meta/Amazon/Keppel/Telin Pacific cable — US West Coast to Singapore via Guam, Philippines and Indonesia. RFS Oct 2025. Main trunk 16,556 km.

name: Echo
description: Google/Meta transpacific cable — Singapore (Changi/Telstra) to US West Coast (Eureka CA) via Indonesia and Guam. Main trunk 16,206 km. RFS 2024–25.

name: Topaz
description: Google private cable — US Pacific Northwest (Vancouver/Port Alberni BC) to Japan (Takahagi/Ibaraki + Shima/Mie). ~10,000 km. RFS Jun 2024.

name: Tabua
description: Google (Starfish) South Pacific cable — Los Angeles to Sydney/Queensland via Hawaii and Fiji. Main trunk 13,323 km. RFS Q1 2026.

name: Japan Guam Australia
description: Google/Telstra/KDDI/SoftBank/AT&T transpacific cable connecting Japan to Australia via Guam and Darwin. ~9,200 km. RFS 2020.

name: SEA-US
description: Southeast Asia – United States cable connecting Philippines and Guam to Hawaii and Los Angeles. ~15,000 km. Consortium: Globe, PLDT, Telstra, Teleguam, Hawaiian Telcom.

name: Southeast Asia Japan Cable 2
description: High-capacity cable connecting Singapore, Philippines, Taiwan, Japan and South Korea. ~10,500 km. Owners: NTT, SoftBank, PLDT, Globe, Chunghwa, Singtel, KT. RFS 2021.

name: Asia Direct Cable
description: Regional intra-Asia cable connecting Japan, Taiwan, Hong Kong, Philippines and Singapore. ~6,500 km. Owners: NTT, Chunghwa Telecom, PCCW, PLDT, StarHub.

name: JUNO
description: Japan–US trans-Pacific cable connecting Minamiboso (Chiba) and Shima (Mie) to Grover Beach, California. 11,710 km. Owner: Seren Juno. Supplier: NEC. RFS May 2025.

name: Proa
description: Google-owned regional cable connecting Shima (Japan), Tinian (Northern Mariana Islands) and Guam. 2,891 km. Supplier: NEC. RFS 2026.

name: Balikula
description: South Pacific cable connecting Brisbane, Vanuatu (Port Vila) and Fiji. ~3,050 km. Google/Telstra. Provides first direct fibre connectivity to Vanuatu.

name: Southern Cross Cable – North
description: Original Southern Cross north route: Sydney (Brookvale) to Hawaii (Kahe Point, Oahu) via Suva, Fiji, then onward to Nedonna Beach, Oregon. ~13,700 km. Owners: Spark NZ (38%), Singtel (30%), Telstra (25%), Verizon (6%). RFS 2000.

name: Southern Cross Cable – South
description: Original Southern Cross south route: Sydney (Alexandria) to Morro Bay, California via Auckland (Takapuna) and Hawaii (Spencer Beach, Big Island). ~14,400 km. Same consortium as SCC_N. RFS 2000.

name: Southern Cross NEXT
description: Upgraded transpacific cable: Hermosa Beach (LA) to Sydney and Auckland via Hawaii, with Fiji branch. 15,857 km, 72 Tbps. Same consortium plus ASN supply. RFS July 2022.

name: Jupiter
description: Meta/Amazon consortium transpacific cable — US West Coast (Pacific City, Oregon) to Japan (Chikura/Shima) and Philippines (Daet) via North Pacific branching unit. ~14,500 km. RFS 2020.

name: Apricot
description: Google/Meta/NTT/PLDT/SoftBank consortium cable — Singapore to Japan (Shima/Maruyama) and Guam via Philippines (Daet) and Philippine Sea branching unit. ~12,000 km. RFS 2024.

name: FASTER
description: Trans-Pacific, Bandon OR–Chikura–Shima–Tamsui

name: PC-1 North
description: Trans-Pacific, Harbour Pointe WA–Ajigaura

name: PC-1 South
description: Trans-Pacific, Grover Beach CA–Shima

name: Unity
description: Trans-Pacific, Chikura–Hawaii–Los Angeles

name: Bay of Bengal Gateway
description: Oman–India–Sri Lanka–Malaysia

name: AAE-1
description: Hong Kong–SE Asia–India–Middle East–Europe

name: RNAL
description: Regional NEC Asia Link — ring topology connecting Hong Kong, Korea, Japan and Taiwan via two paths.
